title: Compiled ProcScript Modules
text: Compiled ProcScript Modules The ProcScript that is compiled into a runtime component is not necessarily the same as that defined in the component development object itself. The compiled ProcScript may include: ProcScript modules inherited from modeled entities, fields, or modeled components. Locally defined ProcScript modules. Uniquely-named entries. Entries have component scope, no matter where they are defined, so if you have entries with the same name defined in several places, the last entry encountered by the compiler is the one that is actually used. Resolved IncludeScript. #INCLUDE directives are replaced by the ProcScript in the referenced IncludeScript module. Resolved constants. Constants such <$entname> or <MAXLENGTH> , are replaced by their values. The compiled ProcScript may exclude: Triggers that are defined in modeled entities and fields that disabled in the current component type. For example, the leaveModifiedKey trigger is disabled in dynamic server page components. ProcScript modules of the same name and type that are defined multiple times. For example, if an entry with the same name is defined for several fields, only the entry defined for the last field processed is used. Uniface provides information and navigation facilities for compiled ProcScript modules. This is useful if an error occurs during compilation, or you encounter unexpected runtime behavior and you need to be able to determine where the offending script is defined. For more information, see Compiled Module Information . Another method for getting information about compiled ProcScript is to generate script listing files. These contain a complete listing of the ProcScript in a compiled development object saved to readable ASCII text file. The contents include the resolved constants and script from IncludeScript modules, as well as inherited and local code. For more information, see Script Listings . Was this topic helpful?
